2026022835815387302.full_text:
One thing I genuinely appreciate about Rohit Sharma is this when he dropped Sanju Samson from the final, he didn’t just leave it at that. He actually walked up to Sanju at the ground, took him aside in a corner, and explained the team combination for a couple of minutes. That’s leadership. You may not agree with the decision, but at least the player gets clarity and respect.

But then you see Suryakumar Yadav in the press conference, and the way he responded about Sanju Samson, it just didn’t sound right. It came off dismissive. You can’t be casual or vague when you’re addressing serious questions. There’s a professional way to handle it.

Honestly, both Gambhir and Surya haven’t exactly impressed as speakers. Surya often gives explanations that feel illogical or poor, and Gambhir has already been kept away from press conferences after recent controversies.
